source: All-In Podcast — Trump transition, SEC/crypto regulation, xAI Colossus, AI scaling laws, MicroStrategy, defense tech
summary: Bitcoin will eventually become a serious long-term threat to the US dollar, but BRICS currency is not a credible threat
summary_extended: Baker argues that while BRICS nations will never successfully displace the US dollar as the global reserve currency — primarily because the US rule-of-law framework cannot be replicated by authoritarian regimes — Bitcoin poses a genuinely serious long-term threat to dollar dominance. He frames Bitcoin not as an immediate danger but as a structural force that will grow in relevance over time, particularly as a check on fiscal excess by governments. Baker expresses this as a personal conviction, stating directly that 'Bitcoin will at some point be a serious threat to the US dollar,' though he acknowledges the timeline is long and uncertain. The implication is that Bitcoin's value proposition strengthens as an alternative to both fiat debasement and authoritarian monetary systems, making it preferable to other proposed dollar alternatives.
detail: Baker is clear that BRICS will never replace the dollar due to rule-of-law advantages of the US. However, he believes Bitcoin will at some point be a serious threat to dollar dominance. He frames Bitcoin as a useful check on fiscal excess and preferable to authoritarian alternatives.
confidence_evidence: Baker says 'I do think Bitcoin will at some point be a serious threat to the US dollar and that just is what it is' — stated as conviction but with long/uncertain timeline
quote_fragment: Bitcoin will at some point be a serious threat to the US dollar
